title: Thirty-one cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta July 6
summary: On July 6, YKHC reports thirty-one cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta. title: Birdwatching brings millions of dollars to Alaska
summary: A committed and lucky birdwatcher in Alaska may see an elusive bluethroat north of the Brooks Range, catch a glimpse of the bold markings on a harlequin duck as it zips along an Interior river, encounter all four species of eider in Utqiaġvik, or take in the sounds of thousands of feeding shorebirds in the Copper River Delta. Thousands of birdwatchers flock to Alaska each year, drawn by the chance to check rare and hard-to-find species off a Big Year list. In d...

title: Interested in Running for Office – Candidate Filing Period is coming up 7/15-7/25/2022

title: Alaska on fire: Thousands of lightning strikes and a warming climate put Alaska on pace for another historic fire season
summary: Alaska is on pace for another historic wildfire year, with its fastest start to the fire season on record. By mid-June 2022, over 1 million acres had burned. By early July, that number was well over 2 million acres, more than twice the acreage of a typical Alaska fire season. Rick Thoman, a climate specialist at the International Arctic Research Center in Fairbanks, explains why Alaska is seeing so many large, intense fires this year and how the region’s fire season is changing. Why is Alaska seeing so many fires this year? There isn’t...

title: A Bad Trip in Polar Bear Country
summary: A well-intentioned and affable guide seems to be OK with winging a trip to Alaska's north slope for polar bear viewing. Things go wrong. The post A Bad Trip in Polar Bear Country appeared first on Alaska Magazine .
